title: NEO 3.1 P1-S6 section 4.8 ownership-lease schema
description: Schema for .neo\gates\OWNERSHIP_LEASE.json - the session-spanning ownership record for the section 4.8 concurrency-prevention substrate. A session must HOLD a live lease for its scope before editing the governed custody surface; ownership_lease.ps1 guards every acquire/release/reclaim with an OS named mutex (real, OS-enforced mutual exclusion of the critical section) and records durable ownership here. HONEST custody: an OS mutex cannot span a multi-process NEO session, so the mutex delivers atomic acquisition + real refusal of a SIMULTANEOUS acquirer, while THIS FILE carries the session-long holder/expiry and is principal-writable -> tamper-EVIDENT, not tamper-PROOF. A non-cooperating process that edits the surface without calling acquire is NOT blocked by S6 (that non-bypassability is Option A / S7 filesystem ACLs). root_of_trust stays 'provisional-dev' until then. Append/replace by holder; expired leases are reclaimable (logged reclaimed_from) but a LIVE lease is never stolen.

root_of_trust.description: 'provisional-dev' in dev (the file is principal-writable; the OS mutex is the real layer). 'host-anchored' only once Option A / S7 ACLs the file outside the principal's write scope.

leases.description: Live + recently-reclaimed lease records, one active per scope.

scope.description: The lease scope unit. Reserved token 'GOVERNED-ROOT' = whole governed surface, exclusive against any sub-scope. Any other string = a per-slice/per-scope lease; disjoint scopes run in parallel (4.0 hybrid), same scope is serialized.

holder.description: Caller-supplied STABLE session identity (not the pid; pid is metadata). Only this holder may release/refresh its own lease.

pid.description: Metadata: the OS pid of the acquiring process (short-lived; informational only).

expiry.description: ISO-8601 UTC expiry. After this, the lease is reclaimable (no deadlock); before it, the scope is held (bounded wait).

reclaimed_from.description: Set when this lease reclaimed an EXPIRED prior lease: the prior holder id + time. Reclaim is always LOGGED here, never a silent steal; a LIVE lease is never reclaimed.
